content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 4, 2006

George T. Riley
1198 Castro Road
Monterey, CA 93940

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-069

Dear Mr. Riley:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You stated that you have been a member on a committee of the League of Women Voters, Monterey County for the past year, researching and developing a model ordinance for local governments which provides campaign finance and conflict-of-interest regulations for city councils and the board of supervisors. The focus has been on contribution limits, expanded disclosure, and recusal requirements related to contributors and enforcement.

While we appreciate your interest in informing the California government, we have determined that it is not appropriate to provide you specific advice regarding the substance of your proposed local ordinance. Therefore, your request is withdrawn. As a general rule, a local agency may impose additional requirements on persons if the requirements do not prevent a person from complying with the Act. (Sections 81013 and 81009.5.) We may respond to questions regarding the current Political Reform Act. Your letter does not ask any specific questions concerning the Act.

_____________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-06-069
Page No. 2


If you have any specific questions concerning the current Political Reform Act,
you may contact our offices at (916) 322-5660 for assistance.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-069

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice on developing a model ordinance for local governments regarding campaign finance and conflict-of-interest regulations, including contribution limits, expanded disclosure, and recusal requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined it was not appropriate to provide specific advice on the substance of the proposed local ordinance and withdrew the request, noting that as a general rule local agencies may impose additional requirements if they do not prevent compliance with the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (File No. W-06-069). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; general content and guidance provided instead. The FPPC declines to advise on the substance of a proposed ordinance but offers general principles and directs requestor to contact for specific Act-related questions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined it was not appropriate to provide specific advice on the substance of the proposed local ordinance and withdrew the request, noting that as a general rule local agencies may impose additional requirements if they do not prevent compliance with the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew a request for advice from a League of Women Voters member seeking guidance on developing a model local ordinance for campaign finance and conflict-of-interest regulations, determining that the request did not contain specific questions about the Political Reform Act itself.
